project.title: Nam Lot enamel proteome: part of a multi-proxy approach to exploring Homo sapiens arrival, environments and adaptations in Southeast Asia
project.projectDescription: Here we analyse two enamel protein extractions from a single Late Pleistocene dental fragment from Nam Lot (Laos). The proteome is analyzed for potential phylogenetic information, from which we concude the specimen represents an individual belonging to the genus Pongo, and characterized in terms of its molecular damage (deamidation, peptide lengths).
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Two enamel samples were demineralized in hydrochloric acid (1.5 M HCl), without subsequent solubilization or proteolysis using a protease. Peptides in the supernatant were desalted by solid-phase-extraction using C18 stagetips, vacuum dried, and reconstituted in 0.1 % TFA 5 % ACN. Peptides were loaded and separtaed on an EASY-nLC 1200 (Thermo Scientific) and two different gradients using 0.1 % TFA 99.9 % water as solvent A and 0.1 % TFA 80 % ACN 19.9 % water as solvent B at a flow rate of 250 nL/min. The gradien 77 min gradient increased from 5 to 30 % B over 50 min, to 45 % B at  60 min, to 80 % B at 62 min until  67. % B was reduced to 5 % by minute 72. LC was interfaced with an Orbitrap Q-Exactive HF-X (Thermo Scientific) acquiring full MS scans from 350 to 1400 m/z, at 120,000 resolution at 200 m/z, 3e6 AGC target, and 25 ms max. injection time (IT), followed by 10 data-dependent MS2 scans with 1.2 m/z isolation width and HCD fragmentation at a normalized collision energy of 28 and measured at 60,000 resolution, 2e5 AGC target, 118 ms max. IT, and a first mass of 100 m/z. Laboratory blanks were prepared using the same procedure, and included alongside MS/MS analysis as a separate injection.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The raw files were analyzed with Maxquant (version 1.6.3.4) and searched against an in-house curated database containing protein sequences expected in the Hominidae enamel proteome. Enzyme mode was set to “unspecific” and allowed for up to 3 of the following variable modifications: deamidation (NQ), hydroxyproline (P), phosphorylation (STY), oxidation (MW), dioxidation (MW), ornithine formation, pyro-Glu formation (QE). The results were interpreted by a BLAST search to confirm sequence uniqueness. Deamidation calculations and phylogenetic information was performed and retrieved within R, as explained in the manuscript.
